title: assistant-security Skill Behavior Eval Fixtures
description: Provider-neutral offline eval cases for the assistant-security skill's scoped, evidence-based security analysis.
recommended_use: Run each case with the assistant-security skill instructions loaded.
Compare the local response against scope, analyze, report, and evidence requirements.
Use deterministic substring checks as offline proxies for contract adherence, not semantic grading.

cases.title: Threat model scopes before analysis
cases.purpose: Checks that a security request resolves analysis type and scope, loads the right methodology, and avoids whole-project speculation.
cases.prompt: Security review this new OAuth callback endpoint before we ship it.
setup_context: The assistant-security skill instructions are active.
The user mentions a new OAuth callback endpoint.
No specific file path or implementation diff has been supplied.
expected_behavior: Resolves the analysis type as threat model or code review based on the requested endpoint review.
Defines or asks for a concrete scope before claiming findings.
Uses the relevant methodology such as STRIDE for threat modeling or OWASP for code review.
Identifies blockers or missing context if endpoint code is unavailable.
Does not invent vulnerabilities without evidence.
Reports status, risk summary, residual risk, and blockers when confidence is limited.
pass_criteria: The response names the analysis type.
The response identifies scope and missing files or endpoint details.
The response names the security methodology.
The response avoids speculative findings without evidence.
The response lists blockers or follow-up needed for confidence.
fail_signals: Audits the whole project without confirmation.
Invents specific vulnerabilities without code or config evidence.
Reports high confidence despite missing endpoint details.

cases.title: Findings include severity impact remediation
cases.purpose: Checks that security findings are actionable, evidence-based, consistently ranked, and paired with remediation and prioritized action items.
cases.prompt: Audit this diff for SQL injection and auth bypass risks. Report anything medium or higher.
setup_context: The assistant-security skill instructions are active.
The review scope contains a supplied diff with authentication and database access changes.
expected_behavior: Reports only findings supported by specific code, config, dependency, endpoint, or threat path evidence.
Uses the four-level severity scale consistently.
For every finding, includes severity, category, description, impact, evidence, and remediation.
Provides a risk_level that matches the highest material remaining risk.
Orders action_items by priority for findings at or above the medium threshold.
Includes residual risk and explicitly notes any scoped areas not assessed.
pass_criteria: Every finding has severity, impact, evidence, and remediation.
Severity uses critical, high, medium, low, or none consistently.
Action items cover all findings at or above threshold.
Risk level matches the material findings.
Speculative observations are removed or clearly marked as residual risk.
fail_signals: Omits action items for medium or higher findings.
